rationale: Confirmed US NATO asset reductions remain the dominant concern for Romania's Black Sea flank, while Iran deal progress offers a mild indirect positive on energy costs.

top_events.title: US to cut air and naval assets deployed for NATO operations in Europe
top_events.summary: Reduced US NATO presence directly affects Romania's Black Sea security posture, increasing exposure on the eastern flank adjacent to the Ukraine conflict.

top_events.title: Iran says deal with US has 'never been closer'
top_events.summary: Progress toward a US-Iran deal could ease global energy prices, providing indirect relief to Romanian consumers still dealing with elevated cost of living.

top_events.title: Pro-Russian opposition party calls for Armenian election result annulment
top_events.summary: Political instability in Armenia is a weak but real signal of continued Russian influence operations in the post-Soviet space, relevant to Romania's broader regional security awareness.
